title: Clean Code
author: Robert C. Martin
description: A handbook of agile software craftsmanship that guides developers to write cleaner, more readable, and highly maintainable code. Perfect for developers who want to refine their techniques.

title: You Don't Know JS: Scope & Closures
author: Kyle Simpson
description: An essential guide to Javascript's core mechanics. This book breaks down scope, closures, lexical environment, and hoisting in a clear, deep, and understandable way.

title: Designing Data-Intensive Applications
author: Martin Kleppmann
description: A deep dive into database internals, distributed systems, storage engines, data models, and caching strategies. Crucial reading for modern backend software engineers.

title: The Pragmatic Programmer
author: Andrew Hunt & David Thomas
description: A timeless classic that details the core principles of software engineering. Learn to build software that is robust, flexible, self-documenting, and easy to maintain.

title: A Brief History of Time
author: Stephen Hawking
description: An exploration of the origins and structure of the universe. Hawking breaks down complex topics like black holes, space-time, gravity, and the Big Bang theory for general readers.

title: Cosmos
author: Carl Sagan
description: A journey through the cosmic ocean, discovering the history of science, humanity, and our position in the universe. Sagan shares the sheer beauty of astrophysic discovery.

title: The Selfish Gene
author: Richard Dawkins
description: A classic work on evolutionary biology. Dawkins explains the gene-centric view of evolution and how it explains animal behavior, altruism, and cooperation in nature.

title: Astrophysics for People in a Hurry
author: Neil deGrasse Tyson
description: A quick, witty, and entertaining guide to the cosmos. Perfect for busy people who want to understand dark matter, quantum mechanics, and the universe's evolution.

title: The Hobbit
author: J.R.R. Tolkien
description: The marvelous adventure of Bilbo Baggins as he seeks to win a share of the treasure guarded by Smaug. The classic epic fantasy that serves as a prelude to The Lord of the Rings.

title: To Kill a Mockingbird
author: Harper Lee
description: A Pulitzer Prize-winning novel about growth, racial injustice, and compassion in the American South. Atticus Finch's defense of a black man is a timeless moral lesson.

author: George Orwell
description: A dystopian social science fiction masterpiece that explores the dangers of totalitarianism, surveillance, brainwashing, and absolute state control over free thought.

title: The Alchemist
author: Paulo Coelho
description: A magical fable about Santiago, an Andalusian shepherd boy, who yearns to travel in search of worldly treasure. His quest teaches him about listening to his heart and following dreams.
